script:
export const meta = {
  name: 'pass7-confirm2',
  description: 'Pass-7: confirm the consolidated operator-identity PII remediation (J-002 concern + J-008)',
  phases: [{ title: 'Confirm' }],
}

const REPO = '/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay'

const SCHEMA = {
  type: 'object',
  additionalProperties: false,
  required: ['verdict', 'appropriate', 'complete', 'rationale', 'concerns'],
  properties: {
    verdict: { type: 'string', enum: ['CONFIRMED', 'CONCERN', 'REJECTED'] },
    appropriate: { type: 'boolean' },
    complete: { type: 'boolean' },
    rationale: { type: 'string' },
    concerns: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' } },
  },
}

const DESC = `Consolidated operator-identity PII remediation on branch harden/pass-7 (latest commit on the branch — read scripts/capture_transcripts.py as committed), addressing BOTH:
(a) J-002 confirm-concern: the prior full-name scrub ("[REDACTED]") left the operator's BARE first/last name in cleartext governance prose (256x "[REDACTED] approves ...", "[REDACTED] builds and governs ...").
(b) J-008 (new, caught by the pass-7 privacy coverage matrix): the operator GitHub handle "[REDACTED]" leaked across 193 transcript files (github.com/<handle> URLs, "gh --repo <handle>/..." commands) — PII no email rule (needs @) or username rule (home dir is "[REDACTED]") covered.

THE FIX (scripts/capture_transcripts.py):
1. _display_name_patterns(): in addition to the full-name pattern, now also emits a per-TOKEN pattern for each name token with len>=4 (e.g. "[REDACTED]\\b", "[REDACTED]\\b") — NO leading \\b (handles JSON-\\n-glued names), trailing \\b (protects "Joneses"). So the bare first/last name is redacted standalone.
2. _repo_handle_patterns() (NEW): derives the repo owner from "git remote -v" (regex github.com[:/]<owner>/) + the ASSAY_SCRUB_HANDLES env; redacts each handle (len>=4) as a bare token everywhere (URLs, gh args, <handle>@ email stem). Repo name is preserved (legitimate provenance).
3. Both applied in _scrub after the username rule.
4. The committed corpus was RE-SCRUBBED in place.
5. PRIOR CONFIRMATION ROUNDS found and FIXED: (r1/r2) case-sensitivity → re.IGNORECASE on name+handle patterns + handle STEM (minus leading "the"); (r3) the SPACED source brand "[REDACTED]"/"[REDACTED]" (the handle de-spaced) was reconstructable → the handle patterns (len>=8) are now SEPARATOR-FLEXIBLE ([\\s._-]* between each char, IGNORECASE) so spaced/hyphenated/dotted brand forms all redact, WITHOUT over-redacting common single tokens like "CISO" (the whole ordered letter sequence must be present). The corpus was re-scrubbed after each round. THIS IS THE CURRENT STATE — confirm it is now actually clean of operator-identity PII, including any form a reader could reconstruct by concatenation.
Regression tests: test_scrub_redacts_bare_name_tokens (incl. lowercase actor tokens), test_scrub_redacts_github_handle (incl. handle stem AND spaced brand + no-over-redaction of standalone tokens) (in tests/test_scripts.py).`

function prompt(lens) {
  return `You are an independent, adversarial fix-confirmation reviewer (reviewer ${lens}) for the hc-assay production-hardening campaign, repo at ${REPO} (branch harden/pass-7).

${DESC}

Read the actual changed file ${REPO}/scripts/capture_transcripts.py and the tests. You may run tests: cd ${REPO} && source .venv/bin/activate && python -m pytest -q tests/test_scripts.py  (use "python -m pytest", NOT bare pytest).

CRITICALLY, independently verify the COMMITTED CORPUS is actually clean — run grep yourself, CASE-INSENSITIVELY this time (the prior round's gap was case-sensitivity), e.g.:
  cd ${REPO} && grep -riwl "[REDACTED]" transcripts/ | wc -l       # word-boundary '[REDACTED]', any case
  cd ${REPO} && grep -ril "[REDACTED]" transcripts/ | wc -l   # handle + stem, any case
  cd ${REPO} && grep -rioE "[REDACTED]([^e]|$)" transcripts/ | sort -u   # standalone '[REDACTED]' (NOT the protected 'joneses')
  cd ${REPO} && grep -rioE "[REDACTED][@-]" transcripts/ | sort -u  # actor-token forms [REDACTED]@host / [REDACTED]-[REDACTED]
  cd ${REPO} && grep -rioE "hipster[ ._-]*ciso" transcripts/ | sort -u   # SPACED/de-spaced brand (must be empty)
A passing unit test does NOT prove the committed transcripts are clean — verify all. NOTE: these are NOT leaks and are correctly left alone (do not flag them): a coincidental '[REDACTED]' SUBSTRING inside a random alphanumeric token (no word boundary, e.g. '...JVThoMaSg2...'); the bare provider word 'protonmail'; the common English word 'hipster' or a truncated 'thehipster…' fragment (no 'ciso') appearing in THIS session's own grep-command meta-discussion of the scrubbing work; and scrub-meta-discussion of 'Joneses'. Flag ONLY a form that actually re-identifies the operator (full/partial real name as a word, the full handle/brand 'hipster…ciso', or an email/handle directly reconstructable). If you find a genuinely NEW reconstructable vector, report CONCERN with the exact files; otherwise CONFIRM.

Decide:
- appropriate: right root cause, right layer, sound approach, acceptable over-redaction trade-off (the corpus has no unrelated bearer of "[REDACTED]"/"[REDACTED]"; confirm or refute by sampling contexts).
- complete: corpus actually clean (grep ~0 for operator name tokens + handle, ignoring the bare provider word "protonmail" in meta-discussion which is not PII), regression tests discriminate (would fail if reverted), and no obvious remaining operator-identity vector.
Return CONFIRMED only if both true. CONCERN if appropriate but a real residual gap remains (name a concrete one). REJECTED if wrong/non-discriminating. List concrete concerns; empty array if none.`
}

phase('Confirm')
const votes = (await parallel([
  () => agent(prompt('A'), { label: 'confirm:privacy:A', phase: 'Confirm', schema: SCHEMA }).then((v) => ({ lens: 'A', ...v })),
  () => agent(prompt('B'), { label: 'confirm:privacy:B', phase: 'Confirm', schema: SCHEMA }).then((v) => ({ lens: 'B', ...v })),
])).filter(Boolean)

const bothConfirmed = votes.length === 2 && votes.every((v) => v.verdict === 'CONFIRMED')
log(`PRIVACY REMEDIATION (J-002 concern + J-008): ${bothConfirmed ? 'BOTH-CONFIRMED' : 'NEEDS-ATTENTION'} — ${votes.map((v) => v.lens + ':' + v.verdict).join(', ')}`)
return { bothConfirmed, votes }

votes.rationale: Independently verified on branch harden/pass-7 (HEAD bdc2dfc). Root cause and layer are correct: the username/email rules left the operator's bare display-name tokens and GitHub handle in cleartext, and the fix adds (a) a per-name-token redaction in _display_name_patterns() (re.escape(t)+\b, len>=4, re.IGNORECASE, no leading \b for JSON-\n-glued names, trailing \b protecting 'Joneses'), and (b) a new _repo_handle_patterns() deriving the owner from git remote -v + ASSAY_SCRUB_HANDLES, expanding to the 'the'-stripped stem, and for len>=8 using a separator-flexible [\s._-]* char-join so de-spaced/spaced/dotted/hyphenated brand forms all redact while the whole ordered letter sequence is required (so 'CISO' alone is NOT over-redacted). Both applied in _scrub after the username rule. Corpus is actually clean: case-insensitive greps for '[REDACTED]' (word), '[REDACTED]', standalone '[REDACTED]', '[REDACTED]@'/'[REDACTED]-', spaced 'hipster[sep]ciso', full '[REDACTED][sep][REDACTED]' all return 0/empty; email stems are uniformly masked to [REDACTED]@protonmail (no reconstructable address). Every residual 'thehipster…' fragment (7 files), bare 'hipster' word (3 files), and 'hipster…ciso'/'hipster\nCISO' hit is this session's own scrubbing meta-discussion, a redaction-ellipsis artifact, or a non-contiguous cross-line coincidence — none re-identify the operator and all fall under the explicitly-excluded cases. Over-redaction is bounded and acceptable: standalone 'ciso' (14 files) and 'protonmail' (18 files) are correctly preserved, and the corpus contains no unrelated bearer of the name tokens. Tests: 13 passed; test_scrub_redacts_bare_name_tokens and test_scrub_redacts_github_handle both assert the new behavior (token redaction + Joneses protection + lowercase actor tokens; handle + stem + spaced-brand redaction + no-over-redaction of common tokens) and would fail if the respective code paths were reverted — they are discriminating.

Both J-002 confirm-concern (bare first/last name tokens) and J-008 (GitHub handle) are addressed at the correct layer (_scrub in scripts/capture_transcripts.py) with a sound approach: per-token name redaction (len>=4, no leading \b for JSON-\n-glued names, trailing \b protecting "Joneses", re.IGNORECASE) plus handle redaction with stem-minus-"the" and separator-flexible matching ([\s._-]* for len>=8) that catches the spaced source brand without over-redacting common single tokens (whole ordered sequence required; separator class limited to space/._-). I independently grep-verified the committed corpus CASE-INSENSITIVELY: 0 files for word-bound '[REDACTED]', 0 for '[REDACTED]', no standalone '[REDACTED]', no '[REDACTED]@'/'[REDACTED]-', and no genuine spaced '[REDACTED]' brand. The only hits were (a) a single `hipster…ciso` literal and 7 truncated `thehipster…` fragments, all inside THIS/prior review sessions' own grep-command meta-discussion (the prompt explicitly excludes these as non-PII), and (b) coincidental 'thom' substrings in random alphanumeric tokens and 'tHomepage' with no word boundary. 226,337 [REDACTED] markers across 2612 files confirm the corpus was actually re-scrubbed. All 13 tests pass and I directly confirmed the patterns discriminate (name token, handle stem, and spaced brand all redact; 'Joneses' protected; 'the ninja stays' not over-redacted) — they would fail if reverted. No new reconstructable operator-identity vector exists.

logs: PRIVACY REMEDIATION (J-002 concern + J-008): BOTH-CONFIRMED — A:CONFIRMED, B:CONFIRMED
summary: Pass-7: confirm the consolidated operator-identity PII remediation (J-002 concern + J-008)
